sources.use: NHS App embedded header suppression, traffic recognition, SSO return and consent-decline recovery expectations.
NHS App host capabilities, back-action and host navigation posture.
NHS login SSO posture, silent sign-in parameter behavior, and one-time asserted identity exchange constraints.
NHS login button visibility, user agreement to share information, and partner session-management responsibility.
Plain English and NHS service copy tone.
Recovery wording structure and assertive error posture without turning the entry corridor into a form.
Error timing and non-destructive field/content handling.
Automated accessibility scan limits and focused accessibility assertions.
ARIA snapshot evidence for the state card and action cluster.
Narrow embedded viewport, mobile, locale, timezone, and reduced-motion emulation.
Trace zip evidence for successful shell handoff and recovery branches.
Landmark count and labelled region structure.
Visible text as accessible names for buttons and regions.
Compact high-density interface framing, restrained surfaces, and clear information hierarchy.
Route layout thinking and persistent shell separation.
Dense toolbar/action placement conventions and compact row rhythm.

designThesis: A compact secure vestibule: one card, one status transition area, a low action cluster, and immediate handoff into the existing embedded patient shell only after local session continuity is safe.
